title: Number of clinical trials
subtitle: Annual number of completed clinical trials registered in the [ClinicalTrials.gov](#dod:clinical-trials-gov) database for each country. Registration in the ClinicalTrials database in the is mandatory for trials in the United States, but voluntary for other countries.
note: ClinicalTrials.gov was established in 2000 and data on trials starting before that is incomplete. There can also be a delay in registration, so latest years might not have complete data yet.
caption:
Number of clinical trials

Annual number of completed clinical trials registered in the ClinicalTrials.gov database for each country. Registration in the ClinicalTrials database in the is mandatory for trials in the United States, but voluntary for other countries.

This line chart tracks the annual number of completed clinical trials registered in the ClinicalTrials.gov database from 2000 to 2024 for a selection of countries. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 7,000 trials. The United States is represented by a distinct blue line that remains significantly higher than all other plotted nations throughout the timeline. Starting near 300 in 2000, the United States line rises steadily to a peak just under 7,000 around 2019, before fluctuating between 6,000 and 7,000 through 2024. A cluster of other countries, including Turkey, China, Egypt, France, Spain, Italy, Canada, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Brazil, occupies the lower portion of the chart, generally remaining below the 2,500 mark. Within this lower group, Turkey and China show upward trends in the later years, with Turkey reaching approximately 2,000 by 2024 and China nearing 1,800. Most other lines in this cluster stay below 1,000, with several hovering near the bottom axis. The data begins in 2000, consistent with the establishment of the database, though registration delays may affect the completeness of the most recent years.
